mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed negative returns in >55% of cases when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% for at least two consecutive months, invalidating the threshold signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in real personal income and a decline in consumer prices expands real purchasing power, driving higher corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 returns as markets price in earnings revisions.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings reports confirm macroeconomic strength.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240.0, it indicates entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 240.0, negating the inflation threshold effect.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment confirms a robust labor market expansion that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high interest rates, a sustained decline in housing starts signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and related supply chains, dragging down corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns during tightening cycles, with a predictive accuracy below 40% over a 4-year rolling window.
